headline: Agentic coding infrastructure dominated the month as tool calling and system architecture led a broad rotation toward production agents.
body: Tool calling, multilingual speech, embeddings, benchmark, and I/O optimization all topped the movers, with I/O optimization and optimal transport breaking out alongside new technique entrants including feed-forward network, optimization bounds, and Discrete Hartley Transform. The agent-building theme ran across the window: DeepReinforce shipped the Ornith family of open agentic coding models from 9B to 397B with local GGUF support [0], and production talks centered on durable session logs [5], persistent memory and context [3], and continuous online evaluation for autonomous agents [10][2]. Google Cloud added data agents through its Agent Development Kit and Model Context Protocol integrations [9], while recursive inference-time patterns were proposed to improve coding agents [6]. The framing across speakers shifted from writing code to designing agentic systems [11].

cited_items.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
